game_name: Castle of Illusion Starring Mickey Mouse

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures (enchanted mushrooms, toy soldiers, clowns, spiders, armored knights)
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat requiring avoidance or defeat via bounce mechanic

danger_signals.visual_form: Masters of Illusion at level endpoints, visually distinct from regular enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Significant danger checkpoint; defeat required to obtain Rainbow Gem and progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Level-specific obstacles (storm phenomena in 'The Storm' level, desert factory machinery in 'Dessert Factory')
danger_signals.meaning: World-state danger; thematic to each illusion zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Mizrabel as final boss; giant and youthful transformation in witch's tower
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate antagonist threat; represents the central conflict state

safety_signals.visual_form: Apples, marbles, and bonus-granting objects scattered in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; enables player offense and health maintenance

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual rewards granting additional tries and health restoration
safety_signals.meaning: Passage safety markers; buffer against danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Five doors in castle hallway leading to distinct illusion worlds
safety_signals.meaning: Safe checkpoint zones; organized progression through contained threat spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Enchanted creatures and toy soldiers serving witch
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enchanted mushrooms, toy soldiers, ball-juggling clowns on unicycles, spiders, armored knights
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence throughout all illusion levels; organized under Masters of Illusion command

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mizrabel's elite henchmen; each guards a Rainbow Gem
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visually distinct boss characters stationed at each level's endpoint
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Level culmination encounters; thematically aligned with level's illusion type
Castle hallway; dispenses quest objective (find Seven Gems of the Rainbow)
Traverses castle, defeats enemies via bounce mechanic, collects gems

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Witch's tower as final destination; Mizrabel's presence throughout castle as active threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mickey must navigate five levels, defeat Masters of Illusion, obtain seven Rainbow Gems to access witch's tower

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rainbow Gems awarded after boss defeats; final two levels grant gems mid-stage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gem collection unlocks route to witch's tower; progression blocker until all gems obtained

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six distinct illusory worlds: Enchanted Forest, Toyland, The Storm, Dessert Factory, The Library, The Castle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each level features thematic enemies and environmental hazards specific to illusion type

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Easy mode available with truncated first three levels, no boss fights
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mode selection determines threat density and progression complexity

environmental_storytelling.example: Enchanted Forest level communicates nature-based illusion through level environment; Toyland communicates toy-soldier army; Dessert Factory communicates industrial/confectionery danger
Enchanted mushrooms in forest zones, toy soldiers in Toyland, clowns in circus-themed areas; enemy type signals current illusion realm
Masters of Illusion stationed at level endpoints serve as environmental markers of progressing through witch's controlled territories

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Seven Gems of the Rainbow collected across levels
environmental_storytelling.meaning: Communicates increasing proximity to endgame; visual progression toward witch's tower access

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Performs bounce jumps to defeat enemies or reach elevated safety areas
Standard movement patterns within level zones
Respond to Mickey's presence; require bounce collision to defeat
Stationed at level endpoints; defeated to obtain Rainbow Gems and progress
Giant, youthful transformation in witch's tower; final threat requiring gem collection as prerequisite

notes: Castle of Illusion communicates world-state primarily through thematic level design and enemy variety rather than explicit UI markers. The game establishes a clear antagonist faction (Mizrabel's army) versus player alignment through enemy types and positioning. Danger signals are conveyed via creature design and environmental hazards specific to each illusory world. Progression is blocked by gem collection, visually communicating the player's approach to endgame. The game does not depict political situations in traditional sense; rather, it presents a fantasy rescue narrative with clear hero/villain factions. Easy mode availability signals difficulty state and player accessibility communication.
